title: Ruth: Redemption in Bethlehem
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of ruth: redemption in bethlehem and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study ruth: redemption in bethlehem and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What is the Hebrew term for the role Boaz fulfilled in redeeming Naomi's land and marrying Ruth? Answer: kinsman-redeemer, or goel in Hebrew.
acceptable_variants: goel — the near kinsman who redeems
kinsman redeemer — the family member who buys back land and provides an heir
the goel role of buying back what a family has lost

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Hebrew for kinsman-redeemer; the family member with the right and responsibility to buy back property, marry a widow, and redeem what was lost; Boaz fulfills this role for Naomi and Ruth
A member of the nation east of the Dead Sea descended from Lot; Ruth is a Moabite who joins Israel through faith in Yahweh and becomes an ancestor of David and Jesus
